title: Orchis tephrosanthos
intro: Orchis tephrosanthos, also known as the "Macedonian Orchid," is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Orchis tephrosanthos include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, such as tephrosin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of folk remedies, such as poultices and salves.
taxonomy: Orchis tephrosanthos is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Further classification places it in the genus Orchis and the subgenus Orchis. Within the subgenus, it is a member of the section Orchis. Orchis tephrosanthos is a species of orchid native to southern Europe and North Africa.
